model_profile.usage: Use this document as the user prompt or append it with --rules. Do not pass it through --system-prompt-override, because that replaces Grok Build's own agent, tool, permission, and repository instructions.

tuning.rule: Use high reasoning effort. Official xAI documentation says Grok 4.5 reasoning cannot be disabled.
Do not send presencePenalty, frequencyPenalty, or stop; the reasoning endpoint does not support them.
The model accepts 500,000 context tokens, but pricing changes above 200,000. Keep active context below that boundary through targeted reads and durable audit packets.
Parallel tool calls are useful for independent reads and reader scopes. Serialize repository edits, evidence publication, commits, and pushes.
Use closed schemas for machine output and validate them locally. Do not infer success from fluent prose.

model_profile.recommended_powershell_launch: $env:GROK_SUBAGENTS = '1' grok --no-auto-update ` --cwd C:\Users\charl\midnight-cardano-bridge ` --model grok-4.5 ` --effort high ` --permission-mode bypassPermissions ` --check ` --output-format plain ` --prompt-file .\docs\grok-4.5-handoff.xml
model_profile.launch_notes: Run grok inspect first and honor every discovered AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, skill, hook, plugin, and MCP rule. Keep instructions short in any new AGENTS.md because xAI documents that short, specific project rules are followed more reliably. Use subagents only for independent read-only research or review. Keep implementation ownership serial so agents do not edit the same files concurrently. Keep the working context below 200,000 tokens when possible. Read targeted files and summaries instead of loading whole upstream mirrors. The local Grok CLI is 0.2.93. Full permission mode applies to Grok's implementation session. Every Codex audit call remains sandbox=read-only and approval-policy=never.

bridge_handoff.mission: Close Sprint 2 of the Midnight/Cardano recursive proof bridge without inflating structural harness evidence into a chain-proof or deployment claim. Implement the remaining council dispositions, obtain a fresh three-reader closure, refresh bound evidence once, and leave the branch committed, pushed, and reproducible.

repository.identity_rule: At startup, record git rev-parse HEAD. The commit containing this file cannot embed its own SHA.
repository.rule: Never reset, discard, or overwrite changes you did not author.
Commit coherent remediation waves and push only this feature branch.
Do not merge to the default branch or mark a PR ready without human direction.

repository_map.workspace: This is the only program repository. Run git, tests, OpenSpec, commits, and pushes here.
repository_map.area: The complete 25-section bridge design. Preserve exactly 25 numbered sections and keep present-tense system facts free of iteration history.

Admission rules and status for the blocked 42 Cardano and 52 Midnight predicates. A count is not a catalog. Each admitted row needs source-backed semantics, anchor, witness, output, finality, freshness, and destination-use fields.
Machine-readable schemas, fixtures, domain profiles, and the canonical gate roster.
Rust canonicalization, hashing, classification, and structural contract checks.
Independent deterministic CBOR checks and the parser-only gnark BSB22 boundary.
Python adapters. All public HTTP reads pass through Scrapling and remain unsigned observations unless a separate authenticated proof validates them.
Input-bound golden reports. They are not mutable last-run status and may be refreshed only through explicit update mode after implementation and review state stop changing.
Stable capability specifications and active sprint changes. Repository-local openspec skills and openspec/config.yaml are mandatory.
The approved 11-sprint, 62-package program design and executable plans.
PowerShell setup, comparison, verifier, evidence publication, and regression entrypoints.
Deep Research Toolkit runs with fetched material, extracted claims, and quote-gate output.

repo.name: deep-research-toolkit
repo.role: source ingestion, quote gates, compilation, retrieval, and contradiction checks

repo.name: midnight-docs
repo.role: official Midnight documentation and release constraints

repo.name: midnight-ledger
repo.role: Midnight transaction, state, and ledger semantics

repo.name: midnight-node
repo.role: AURA, GRANDPA, BEEFY, MMR, relay, and runtime implementation

repo.name: midnight-zk
repo.role: Halo2/Plonkish, KZG, aggregation, and ZKIR implementation

repo.name: mithril
repo.role: Cardano certificate chain, AVK evolution, and signed-entity implementation

repo.name: partner-chains
repo.role: Cardano and Substrate interoperability source material

repo.name: cardano-ledger
repo.role: Cardano era, ledger state, serialization, and transition rules

repo.name: plutus
repo.role: Plutus V3 and BLS12-381 execution surface

repo.name: CIPs
repo.role: CIP-0038, CIP-0133, CIP-0165, and related standards text

repo.name: apk-proofs
repo.role: committee-key and aggregate public-key proof research

upstream_mirrors.rule: Use these repositories to trace primary source code and history. Do not edit, commit, or push them as part of bridge work.

tool.name: cargo
tool.note: Not on PATH in the observed shell.

tool.name: rustc
tool.note: Not on PATH in the observed shell.

tool.name: go
tool.note: Not on PATH in the observed shell.

host_environment.warning: The canonical design's dated local probe says Docker is absent, WSL is disabled, and Cardano binaries are available. Those statements no longer match the observed host. Re-probe, preserve command output, and correct the current environment record without treating Docker availability as Midnight or Cardano deployment readiness.

tooling_and_research.openspec: Read AGENTS.md and openspec/config.yaml first. Use the repository skills under .codex/skills/openspec-* for proposal, exploration, application, synchronization, review, and archive work. Validate with npm run openspec:validate.
tooling_and_research.deep_research_toolkit: The toolkit mirror is _external/deep-research-toolkit, its environment is .venv-drt, and project policy is .deepresearch.yml. Durable runs live in research-runs. Use the toolkit's fetch, claim extraction, verbatim quote gate, compiler, retrieval planner, and contradiction checks. Never admit a load-bearing chain claim from model memory.
tooling_and_research.web: Every public web pull uses Scrapling. Record URL, request method and body, retrieval time, response status, content hash, adapter revision, and the exact quote supporting each admitted claim. Prefer specifications, source repositories, release artifacts, and chain-owned documentation.
tooling_and_research.shell: Use PowerShell on Windows. Resolve pinned tools through the setup/verifier functions or the explicit paths above. Do not silently substitute globally installed versions.

agent_workflow_tools.discovery: grok inspect is authoritative for loaded rules, skills, hooks, plugins, permissions, and MCP servers. Run it again after changing configuration. Duplicate skill names can come from user and plugin scopes; follow one current copy, not both in sequence.

skill.name: executing-plans
skill.use: execute the approved written Sprint 2 plan with checkpoints

skill.name: systematic-debugging
skill.use: every unexpected result or failing test

skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.use: evaluate Codex and council findings against code and evidence

skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.use: before any success, commit, or push claim

skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.use: only independent read-only research or reader scopes

skill.name: humanizer
skill.use: every documentation draft and final pass

agent_workflow_tools.tool: Use knowledge-extraction and research-knowledge-graph for admitted claims, knowledge-compiler and retrieval-planner for dossiers and contradictions, and the verbatim quote gate before a chain fact enters the canonical design.
This is the only public web transport for the project. Other agents may propose URLs, but the recorded fetch must pass through Scrapling.

ground_truth.sprint_2_state: Implementation exists; OpenSpec task 6.1 remains open.

non_claims.item: No Cardano validator has accepted the complete wrapped Midnight relation.
No Midnight operation has accepted the external Cardano proof atomically.
The Go BSB22 component is a byte parser, not a proof verifier.
Midnight and Mithril captures are unsigned endpoint observations.
All six S01-BLOCK gates and all eight CONS gates remain open.

preflight.step: Run git status --short, git branch --show-current, git log -8 --oneline, and grok inspect.
Confirm the branch contains 54e8d36, cee2bd2, c81e552, and 9b2d60b.
For every _external child repository used, record its full HEAD SHA, origin URL, branch or detached state, and git status. Do not fetch or update it during preflight.
Run gh auth status without printing token material; record only account, host, and authentication result.
Run grok mcp doctor codex-auditor --json and require healthy=true with two tools discovered.
Record Windows, WSL2, Docker, Compact, cardano-node, cardano-cli, Rust, Go, Python, Node, OpenSpec, Grok, and Codex versions or explicit not-found results.
Run pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts/verify-reference-harness.ps1 and retain its exact output.
If go is absent from PATH, use $HOME/.local/toolchains/go1.25.7/go/bin/go.exe.
If cargo is absent from PATH, use $HOME/.cargo/bin/cargo.exe.
Do not update evidence during preflight. A failure must be investigated before any claim of progress.

codex_audit_channel.purpose: Codex is an independent audit reader. Grok owns implementation; Codex challenges the patch, evidence, tests, and claims. Codex must not edit the repository during an audit.
codex_audit_channel.identity_limit: This channel starts a Codex CLI session on the same machine. It does not connect to the exact live chat that prepared this handoff and it does not inherit hidden conversation state. Every request must carry the repository SHA, scope, acceptance criteria, known non-claims, and commands needed to reproduce the evidence.
codex_audit_channel.project_mcp:
The committed .grok/config.toml registers codex-auditor as a stdio server backed by "codex mcp-server". The local handshake was checked against MCP protocol 2025-06-18; Grok discovered two tools.
Start one audit with prompt set to the complete request packet, cwd set to C:\Users\charl\midnight-cardano-bridge, sandbox set to read-only, approval-policy set to never, and model set to gpt-5.6-sol. Preserve the returned threadId and content. Do not set base-instructions.
Continue only the same audit using its threadId and a complete follow-up prompt. Use this for evidence-backed dispositions and clarification. Start a new codex tool call, with a fresh thread, for the final reread.
If MCP health, authentication, model selection, or a tool call fails, use the CLI fallback below. Do not replace gpt-5.6-sol silently and do not treat a timeout or an empty response as approval.
codex_audit_channel.request_packet: <codex_audit_request version="1"> <audit_id>stable wave or closure identifier</audit_id> <role>Independent read-only bridge proof and implementation auditor</role> <repository>C:\Users\charl\midnight-cardano-bridge</repository> <branch>resolve-checklist-full-sweep</branch> <head_sha>full git rev-parse HEAD value</head_sha> <base_sha>full comparison base SHA</base_sha> <scope>One remediation wave, one bounded diff, or one closure question</scope> <changed_files>Exact git diff --name-status output for the scope</changed_files> <acceptance_criteria>Concrete requirements and failure conditions</acceptance_criteria> <known_non_claims>Open cryptographic, consensus, destination, and deployment gates</known_non_claims> <verification>Commands already run with exit codes and evidence paths</verification> <questions>Specific claims Codex must try to falsify</questions> <response_contract> Findings first, ordered Blocking, Major, Minor. Each finding must include a stable id, file and line, violated requirement, technical consequence, reproduction or inspection method, and minimum acceptable disposition. End with exact B/M/m counts, residual test gaps, and verdict pass or changes-required. Do not edit files. </response_contract> </codex_audit_request>
codex_audit_channel.recording_contract: Store each request, verbatim response, and Grok disposition under openspec/changes/sprint-02-reference-harness-poc/review-evidence/codex/<audit-id>/. For CLI fallback, also store transcript.jsonl and extract the session id from it. Record request, response, and transcript SHA-256 values, Codex threadId or CLI session id, model, repository HEAD, and UTC time. Never rewrite a prior response. A disposition is fixed, rejected with cited evidence, or deferred with human approval. Deferred Blocking or Major findings keep the sprint open.
codex_audit_channel.cli_fallback: $repo = 'C:\Users\charl\midnight-cardano-bridge' $auditId = 'replace-with-stable-audit-id' $auditDir = Join-Path $repo "openspec\changes\sprint-02-reference-harness-poc\review-evidence\codex\$auditId" $requestPath = Join-Path $auditDir 'request.xml' $responsePath = Join-Path $auditDir 'response.md' $transcriptPath = Join-Path $auditDir 'transcript.jsonl' New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $auditDir | Out-Null $prompt = Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $requestPath $prompt | codex -a never exec --ephemeral ` -C $repo ` -s read-only ` -m gpt-5.6-sol ` --json ` --color never ` -o $responsePath ` - | Tee-Object -FilePath $transcriptPath | Out-Null $exitCode = $LASTEXITCODE if ($exitCode -ne 0 -or -not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $responsePath)) { throw "Codex audit failed or produced no response" } Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 -LiteralPath $requestPath, $responsePath, $transcriptPath

cadence.step: Request one bounded Codex audit after each coherent remediation commit.
Answer findings with code, tests, or cited evidence. Do not answer with intent.
Use codex-reply to test dispositions in the same thread.
After all waves, start a fresh Codex audit over the complete closure diff.
The Codex audit supplements the proof, consensus, and operator council. It cannot replace those readers or any chain receipt.

engineering_rules.rule: For every behavior change, add one focused failing regression, run it RED for the expected reason, implement the smallest fix, then run focused and full GREEN verification.
Use apply_patch for manual edits. Preserve unrelated worktree changes.
Use Scrapling for every public web pull. Preserve exact request and response provenance. Admit new design claims only through primary sources and the Deep Research Toolkit evidence gate.
Use the humanizer skill on documentation. Keep technical prose plain, specific, and free of revision narration.
Use structured parsers and closed schemas. Do not rely on ad hoc string matching for security or evidence state.
Never invent predicate rows, chain facts, proof success, testnet receipts, or gate closure.
Default verification is offline, read-only, fail-closed, and temporary-output isolated. Only explicit update mode may publish evidence.
An implementer cannot approve its own work. Fresh readers receive the current snapshot and acceptance criteria, not prior review narration.
Use Codex only as a read-only auditor. Preserve requests, responses, dispositions, thread ids, and hashes. Never summarize away an unresolved finding.

wave.name: telemetry-boundary
wave.problem: OpenSpec telemetry is not explicitly disabled before version discovery and validation.
wave.required_result: Set and restore OPENSPEC_TELEMETRY=0 and DO_NOT_TRACK=1 before every OpenSpec invocation. Test with isolated config and data directories that no telemetry connection is attempted and no user config is written.

wave.name: proof-vector-closure
wave.problem: The CBOR manifest and BSB22 sentinel remediation require an independent technical reread.
wave.required_result: Bind structural-cbor-schema-v1.json to Rust, Go, and the independent cbor2 checker at the field/type level, not only by member counts.

Add a versioned BSB22 proof/VK fixture with a distinct sentinel for every named field. Drive every relevant equal-width proof and VK swap through Parse, compare against the registered named expectation, and return a stable parser-only mismatch code. Bind execution into the combined verifier and evidence.
Keep S01-BLOCK-04/full-decider and S01-BLOCK-06/cardano-execution unresolved and cryptographic_verification=false.

wave.name: evidence-contract
wave.problem: The repository does not state one exact structural-payload/conformance-envelope contract.
wave.required_result: Choose one unambiguous contract. Prefer a structural payload bound by hash from a conformance envelope rather than duplicating the execution manifest. Align OpenSpec, code, tests, and README. Add stale payload, stale envelope, and mixed-generation negatives.

wave.name: command-provenance
wave.problem: Evidence records descriptive command strings, including a non-executable placeholder.
wave.required_result: Generate structured records from the same invocation wrapper that runs each command. Record logical tool, resolved executable identity, pinned version, cwd, argv, relevant offline environment, and inline-source SHA-256. Test that records match actual invocations.

wave.name: python-artifact-lock
wave.problem: The Python lock pins versions but not artifacts.
wave.required_result: Commit hashes for every accepted Windows CPython 3.14 artifact, install with pip --require-hashes and binary-only policy, and test target-platform and hash completeness. Rebuild the venv from that lock before accepting it.

wave.name: crash-safe-publication
wave.problem: Two sequential file replacements are not a crash-atomic evidence transaction and depend on TEMP sharing the repository volume.
wave.required_result: Stage an immutable generation on the destination volume, verify the full pair, and publish a small current-generation manifest last. Readers must reject missing, mixed, or incomplete generations. Test second-write failure, interruption before pointer publication, startup recovery, and a TEMP directory on another volume or an equivalent injected path-root condition.

wave.name: control-and-bootstrap-evidence
wave.problem: The canonical verifier omits its PowerShell control tests and a real clean-checkout bootstrap qualification.
wave.required_result: Run control tests without recursive verifier invocation. Add a registry-backed clean-checkout qualification as a separate, explicitly networked dependency-bootstrap phase; keep public-chain endpoints unreachable. Bind a deterministic qualification receipt into offline conformance.

wave.name: minor-operator-fixes
wave.required_result: WhatIf setup reports PLANNED or ready=false. READY is reserved for a completed install and exact lock match.
The standalone comparison validates exact Cargo, Rust, and Go versions or consumes a previously validated tool manifest.
The combined verifier runs and binds the late-failure regression.

wave.name: host-capability-reconciliation
wave.problem: The canonical design's dated host probe no longer matches the machine: WSL2 and Docker now work, while Cardano binaries were not found.
wave.required_result: Repeat the host probes from a clean PowerShell process and inside Ubuntu-26.04. Preserve exact commands, paths, versions, and failures in a dated machine-readable receipt.
Update the canonical 25-section design, README, harness environment contract, and any affected OpenSpec text to state the current capabilities. Keep versioned historical evidence immutable.
Do not mark Compact, the Midnight proof server, cardano-node, cardano-cli, or either testnet deployment ready until install, version, configuration, connectivity, and a minimal functional probe pass.

closure_protocol.step: After all remediation waves pass, commit the implementation snapshot.
For every coherent remediation commit, preserve a bounded Codex request, response, disposition, threadId, and hashes. No Codex Blocking or Major finding may remain unresolved.
Start a fresh Codex closure audit against the final implementation snapshot. A same-thread follow-up is not a fresh reread.
Dispatch fresh proof, consensus, and operator readers in parallel. They may write only their own report files.
Require file-and-line citations, commands, evidence, disposition, and exact Blocking/Major/Minor counts.
Resolve every Blocking and Major finding with TDD, then request a fresh reread. Do not downgrade a finding merely to close the sprint.
Create openspec/changes/sprint-02-reference-harness-poc/review.md using the repository OpenSpec review instructions. Record SHA-256 for each final reader report.
Mark task 6.1 complete only when all three final reports have Blocking=0 and Major=0.
Refresh evidence after review artifacts and task state stop changing, then run the default verifier byte-identically.
Archive Sprint 2 only if OpenSpec archive instructions permit it and all stable-spec merges validate. Otherwise leave the reviewed change active.
Commit and push resolve-checklist-full-sweep. Do not merge it.

acceptance_gates.gate: All focused, component, integration, and full verification commands exit zero.
A failed or interrupted run publishes no pass/deployment label and cannot select a partial generation.
Default verification performs no network request and writes no user-global configuration.
Fresh proof, consensus, and operator counts are Blocking=0 and Major=0.
The fresh Codex closure audit reports Blocking=0 and Major=0, and every earlier Codex finding has a preserved evidence-backed disposition.
Strict OpenSpec validation passes after review/task changes.
The canonical design still has exactly 25 numbered sections and contains no iteration narration.
Deployment remains blocked and activation_eligible=false unless real destination-chain receipts and every roster gate exist. This sprint is not expected to change that state.

bridge_handoff.final_response_schema:
Return one XML document with this shape:
complete|blocked full SHA URL or not-pushed reason concise list of actual changes failing tests observed, then passing commands
exact S01-BLOCK and CONS state
facts only

reference.purpose: parallel function calling and tool choice

probe.result: read-only sandbox, ephemeral sessions, model selection, cwd, and output-last-message supported
CODEX_MCP_OK using read-only and approval-policy never
AUDIT_CHANNEL_OK using gpt-5.6-sol, read-only, approval never, and ephemeral mode

local_integration_probes.note: These probes establish transport and configuration only. They do not approve the bridge implementation.

bridge_handoff.system_card_status: On 2026-07-10, Scrapling checks of the official xAI safety page and sitemap found Grok 4, Grok 4 Fast, and Grok Code Fast 1 model cards, but no separate Grok 4.5 model or system card. Use the Grok 4 card only as historical background. Do not transfer its capability, safety, or evaluation claims to Grok 4.5.
